final.summary:
Tailoring log for: Civil Engineer.

- Profile summary: updated toward role; favor concrete systems and evidence from the resume.
- Project id 21: light edit to one part of the project (minor).
- Skills id 76: minor name/category touch-up.
- Skills id 77: minor name/category touch-up.
- Skills id 78: minor name/category touch-up.
- Skills id 79: minor name/category touch-up.

tailorExplanation.paragraph: I treated this as a stretch match for Civil Engineer at Army Civilian Careers, so I focused on transferable proof instead of pretending every JD term was already in your background. The strongest direct overlap I found was engineer; the rest of the draft bridges from nearby evidence where your resume supports it. Use the strongest supported evidence to create an honest transferable fit story. The biggest help here: kept the projects that best prove the role fit, gave the summary a clearer role-specific angle, and moved the most relevant skills higher. Under the hood, I changed the summary, projects, and skills based on those signals.

details.title: What I prioritized
items: Use the strongest supported evidence to create an honest transferable fit story.
I prioritized direct evidence first, transferable proof, measurable outcomes, and claim caution.

details.title: Why it changed
items: Target story: Open with concrete proof from the resume; no new facts; honor every avoid line.

details.title: Fit risk
items: The posting has many unsupported role terms, so the draft should stay conservative and visibly transferable.
Bridge only from evidenced work and keep unsupported expectations in review language.

details.title: Fit read
items: Stretch fit: be transparent and conservative. Frame transferable strengths, keep useful non-keyword evidence, and explicitly avoid unsupported target-role claims.
Transferable evidence I considered: BitGo Software Engineering Intern and Centi – Personal Finance App.

details.title: Not directly evidenced
items: I did not claim civil engineering structures, corrective action, criteria or specifications, design and construction, project design documents, construction, submittals, and corrective because I could not verify that exact experience in the resume.

details.title: Before you send
items: Do not treat weak keyword overlap as direct experience.
I treated civil engineering structures, correcti as review items, not resume claims, because I could not verify them in the resume.
I did not claim heat transfer, abet, and transcripts because that exact evidence was not in the resume.
Some JD terms were not directly evidenced: civil engineering structures, corrective action, criteria or specifications, and design and construction.

jobStrategy.readerGoal: Use the strongest supported evidence to create an honest transferable fit story.
claimRules: Do not treat weak keyword overlap as direct experience.
Do not claim unsupported terms directly: civil engineering structures, correcti...
Do not claim heat transfer, abet, transcripts unless directly evidenced.
Bridge only from evidenced work and keep unsupported expectations in review lan...

experience.title: Software Engineering Intern
experience.description:
• Built and maintained backend services and REST APIs in Python/Django, supporting secure internal workflows for
• financial and compliance teams.
• Developed React dashboards integrated with Django APIs, improving visibility into backend data workflows and
• operational metrics.
• Collaborated with senior engineers on system design and architecture, contributing to scalable service patterns and
• data modeling.
• Optimized Python data-processing pipelines and PostgreSQL queries, achieving a ~40% reduction in latency.

projects.title: Centi – Personal Finance App
projects.description:
• Developed a full-stack finance application that automates account aggregation and transaction tracking, delivering comprehensive spending insights to users.
• Engineered FastAPI services and PostgreSQL schemas for Plaid integrations, ensuring reliable transaction processing and normalized data storage.
• Created responsive React dashboards for account management and financial analytics, significantly improving user engagement.
• Implemented secure authentication and data handling protocols (OAuth/JWT), safeguarding user financial data.

summary.summary: Detail-oriented engineer with a strong foundation in backend development, specializing in Python and FastAPI. Proven ability to design and implement scalable systems, ensuring compliance and secure data handling. Experienced in creating metrics dashboards and optimizing workflows to enhance operational efficiency.

summary.before: Backend developer specializing in Python and FastAPI, with proven expertise in building scalable backend services and data pipelines. Experienced in system architecture and API development, leveraging SQL for data access and reporting.
summary.after: Detail-oriented engineer with a strong foundation in backend development, specializing in Python and FastAPI. Proven ability to design and implement scalable systems, ensuring compliance and secure data handling. Experienced in creating metrics dashboards and optimizing workflows to enhance operational efficiency.

description.before:
• Developed a full-stack finance application that automates account aggregation and transaction tracking, providing
• users with comprehensive spending insights.
• Designed FastAPI services and PostgreSQL schemas for Plaid integrations, enabling reliable transaction processing and
• normalized data storage.
• Created responsive React dashboards for account management and financial analytics, improving user engagement.
• Implemented secure authentication and data handling protocols (OAuth/JWT), safeguarding user financial data.
description.after:
• Developed a full-stack finance application that automates account aggregation and transaction tracking, delivering comprehensive spending insights to users.
• Engineered FastAPI services and PostgreSQL schemas for Plaid integrations, ensuring reliable transaction processing and normalized data storage.
• Created responsive React dashboards for account management and financial analytics, significantly improving user engagement.
• Implemented secure authentication and data handling protocols (OAuth/JWT), safeguarding user financial data.
